description: xrpl wallet new --save — Generate and save a wallet to the keystore in one step, avoiding the separate wallet import command

userStories.title: wallet new --save and wallet new-mnemonic --save
userStories.description: As a user, I want to save a newly generated wallet to the keystore in one step so I don't have to pipe seed output into a separate import command.
acceptanceCriteria: Add --save flag to src/commands/wallet/new.ts; when passed, calls encryptKeystore and writes <address>.json to the keystore directory after generating the wallet
Add --password <pw> option to wallet new: skips interactive prompt and prints 'Warning: passing passwords via flag is insecure' to stderr
Without --password, prompts interactively for password twice (confirm match) using readline
Add --alias <name> option to wallet new: passed to encryptKeystore as label; applies same alias uniqueness check as wallet import (scan keystoreDir, exit 1 if taken, --force overrides)
Add --keystore <dir> option to wallet new: overrides keystore directory; XRPL_KEYSTORE env var also respected via getKeystoreDir
When --save is used, human-readable output appends line: 'Saved to <filepath>'
When --save is used with --json, output includes keystorePath field
Non-interactive safety: if --save is passed and process.stdin.isTTY is false and --password is not provided, exit 1 with: 'Error: --password is required when --save is used in non-interactive mode'
Existing wallet new behaviour is unchanged when --save is not passed (all existing tests still pass)

Add identical --save, --password, --alias, --keystore flags to src/commands/wallet/new-mnemonic.ts with same behaviour; stores the mnemonic phrase as the keystore secret (same as wallet import does for mnemonics)
E2E test (wallet new): run wallet new --save --password test123 --keystore <tmpdir> --json, assert exit 0, assert <address>.json exists in tmpdir, assert keystorePath field in JSON output
E2E test (wallet new --alias): run with --alias bob --save --password test123 --keystore <tmpdir>, assert keystore JSON contains label: 'bob', assert wallet alias list --keystore <tmpdir> shows bob

E2E test (wallet new-mnemonic): run wallet new-mnemonic --save --password test123 --keystore <tmpdir>, assert keystore file exists, run wallet decrypt-keystore --password test123 --keystore <tmpdir> --json on the address, assert seed field equals the mnemonic from wallet new-mnemonic output
E2E test (non-TTY): run wallet new --save --keystore <tmpdir> with stdin redirected from /dev/null and no --password, assert exit 1 and stderr contains '--password is required'
